test.name: True date conflict (gap matches no calendar offset) should route to conflict-resolution
test.description: Two records list a death date 5 days apart in the same English county in 1865 — well after the Gregorian transition (1752) and a gap that matches no known calendar offset (not 10, 11, or 13 days). This is a genuine date conflict between sources, not a calendar-system difference. Should route to conflict-resolution, not convert-dates.

input.user_message: I have two English records for the same person's death: one says 3 June 1865, the other says 8 June 1865. Same county. Which date is right?

negative.explanation: This is a genuine date conflict (5-day gap matches no calendar offset, both post-1752 Gregorian). convert-dates must stay out of the way. conflict-resolution is the appropriate skill — it handles source disagreements where both records use the same calendar system.

judge_context: Should NOT invoke convert-dates — both dates are already Gregorian and the gap does not match any known calendar offset
Should route to conflict-resolution or answer directly using general GPS reasoning about source proximity and evidence weighing
Should optionally explain why this is NOT a calendar issue (no offset matches, both post-1752 England, same jurisdiction)
Should NOT silently 'convert' one date to make the conflict disappear
